purpose: Per-item flag registry for the deep-research-survey skill. Each proposed improvement is an independently-toggleable flag (SKILL.md `flags: <ids>`), so every item can be ablated in isolation and all ablations run in parallel. test_type tells the harness HOW to verify each item — because not every item is a survey-quality change.

test_types.output_ablation: Run baseline (no flags) vs +this-flag on a topic/scenario chosen for THIS item's regime; judge the artifact. Runnable in parallel.
test_types.structural_check: Not a survey-quality change (operational/format). Verify the implementation is present and well-formed via a deterministic check on the skill files / a transcript.
test_types.needs_human: Requires a human in the loop; not runnable in an automated harness.

items.claim: deterministic Workflow orchestration replaces manual agents
items.test: Phase-3 addendum names Workflow/pipeline + drops the manual scratch-poll machinery; (operational) measure silent-death rate, not survey quality.

items.claim: self-eval rubric gate improves the artifact
items.test: thin draft; baseline ships as-is vs +P0-2 score->fix->rescore; independent THIRD judge scores both; metric = quality delta + scorecard present.
deliberately AMBIGUOUS-scope request; baseline guesses-and-goes vs +P0-3 emits a brief / asks; metric = scope-correct or asked-first rate (a wrong guess is the failure).

items.claim: AttributeTree structured evidence reduces attribution drift
items.test: OPEN-BOOK, heavy load: long source with many confusable values + a multi-paragraph synthesis; free-text vs structured-first; metric = value accuracy. NOTE: prior micro-runs nulled — real regime is full-survey scale.
MULTI-AGENT independent-draft regime (shared ledger -> independent parallel writers vs memory-guided sequential); metric = cross-section inconsistencies. VALIDATED (RESULTS Update 8).
items.result: PROMOTED-TO-BASELINE 2026-06-03 (decision 2026-06-03-01): memory-guided synthesis is now the default method for the large/high-stakes multi-agent synthesis path in phases/phase-4-synthesis.md, superseding the blind UNION merge; quick single-pass surveys unchanged. Flag retained as a no-op alias.

items.claim: separate-model/adversarial verification catches errors the writer misses
items.test: OPEN-BOOK: a source doc + claims that CONTRADICT the source (drifted values, fabricated citation); self-review vs separate-verifier; metric = recall of source-contradictions. (Closed-book micro-runs nulled — this is the right regime.)
flat-ledger vs graph-stratified evidence on a topic with cross-cutting themes; judge rates cross-section synthesis / multi-aspect coverage. Borderline-isolable at micro-scale.
verify the +P2-2 report emits a telemetry footer (tokens/wall-clock/USD); operational, not quality.
requires a human to steer mid-run; not runnable in an automated harness.

items.claim: progressive-disclosure hygiene (concise SKILL.md, ref files, TOC)
items.test: SKILL.md line count < 500; detailed rules in per-phase files; long ref files have a TOC.
verify a per-phase failure check exists and maps to a MAST category at each phase.

items.claim: token-efficient citation audit (locus-targeted reads + materiality triage) cuts Phase-5 audit cost at equal detection
items.test: OPEN-BOOK cost+recall A/B: a survey references set containing >=1 mislabeled-source violation; baseline (whole-source verify, one agent/citation) vs +P3-1 (triage -> locus-targeted verify of load-bearing only); metric = output-token delta AND detection recall of the known violation(s). Win = lower tokens AND identical-or-better detection. See proposals/2026-05-31-citation-audit-token-efficiency.md.
items.result: A/B wf_26ff1cdb-821 (N=1): baseline 88877 tok vs P3-1 24359 tok = 3.65x; 2/2 planted mislabeled-source violations caught (decorative one via page-1 identity probe); 0 tool faults. Promoted into the baseline citation-audit/SKILL.md Phases 1/3 by user directive on this directional N=1 evidence, ahead of the proposal's own multi-topic gate — see decisions/2026-05-31-01. Flag P3-1 retained as a no-op alias; multi-topic confirmation (>=2-3 topics, unlabelled-materiality classification, different model pair) still owed.
